query: null undefined undeclared javascript interview question

competitors.label: MDN undefined
competitors.selectionReason: MDN is the canonical reference for undefined semantics and safe checks such as typeof, which are central to separating undefined from undeclared variables.
theirStrengths: It is authoritative on undefined semantics and on why typeof is the safe check for undeclared identifiers.
theirGaps: It is less interview-shaped on when null is intentional and why undeclared should be treated as a scope or typo bug.

realWorldUseCases: undefined means declared but currently no value, null means intentionally empty, and undeclared means the identifier does not exist in scope. Treat them differently in logic and debugging.
followUpConfusion:
Practical rule
Use null when you intentionally clear/reset a value. Treat undefined as "not provided yet". Treat undeclared as a bug (scope/typo/load-order).

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines undefined accurately, but our page turns the distinction into a debugging rule for logic branches, resets, and scope mistakes.
actionableExamples: MDN still has strong examples around typeof and undefined behavior, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the three-way interview trap: intentional null, natural undefined, and undeclared as a separate runtime bug class.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny typeof-safe-check snippet if we later need a clearer example edge over the canonical docs.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical reference for undefined, but our page is stronger for this interview question because it separates null, undefined, and undeclared as distinct debugging states instead of treating undefined in isolation.
